articles.title: Hearing Loss Is a Family Matter: What Audiologists Should Know
articles.summary:
When I finally committed to wearing my hearing aids consistently, it wasn’t a clinician who pushed me there. It was my children. Watching them watch me — retreating from noisy rooms, missing the joke, going quiet at family gatherings — I recognized my own father. He had hidden his hearing loss for decades, and I had spent years following in ...
